synonyms: Symbiosis is a sustained state of collaboration between two
different types of organisms. It often comes in the form of perpetual
cohabitation of members of different species, usually with benefits to
Symbiosis helps animals transcend obstacles and avert mishap.
In the Great Barrier Reef, many aboriginal as well as exotic species
ive in symbiosis. Clownfish and sea anemones, for example, are
benefactors to each other. Clownfish intervene when other organisms
try to harm the sea anemones. When clownfish feed, the food scraps
are picked up by the anemones before they decompose.
The benefits of symbiosis are seldom unilateral, however. In this
case, the poisonous anemones also deter predators of clownfish, such
as octopuses, in effect setting up a “No Trespassing" sign. Clownfish
usually abstain from venturing too far from sea anemones even when
seduced by prey, for fear that they might perish.
According to biologist Lynn Margulis, symbiosis is an efficacious
though sometimes imperceptible driving force behind evolution.
She regards Darwin’s notion of evolution, driven by competition, as
incomplete, and claims evolution is firmly based on collaboration,
interaction, and mutual dependence among organisms.
In short, symbiosis is by no means abnormal. It pervades the
whole biosphere, in environments as diverse as subterranean caves
and submarine hotspots. It is found among organisms from microbes
to bipeds to quadrupeds. Human beings would soon expire if there
were no trees, which produce the oxygen we breathe, through
photosynthesis. It would be an instance of megalomania to claim
ive in solitude in the universe. Therefore, wild
conservation is vital to the very survival of humans.

Next you come to Jiaxian, a secluded town sun-ounded by
Jiaxian is the original taro capital of Taiwan.
The next stop is Baolai, a hot-spring resort. The water temperature
here is about 50 degrees centigrade; you can drink from as well as
bathe in it. It’s an invaluable “beauty spring”; advocates claim that
the water makes your skin tender. If you have a scar somewhere on

Baolai is the port where rafting at Laonung River starts. This is a four­
River route, a two-star one. It takes about two hours to traverse the
2.5 kilometers of rapids and currents. The best time for rafting is
from May through October. However, consecutive rainy days and the
traffic and imperil motorists. You can avert disaster and ensure free
ocomotion by checking the weather before you go.
From Baolai you proceed to Meishan, the southwestern portal to
Yushan National Park. There’s a tourist center here that provides all
sorts of travel information. You can also visit Taiwan’s first botanical
garden of indigenous plants, of which some 50 different varieties have
been assembled here. It will be a memorable experience.
Meishan is inhabited by the Bunun aborigines, who pursue
traditional farming. There is one majestic suspension bridge to either
side of the village. The view around here is spectacular.
Meishan is the final settlement on the western side of the Central
Mountain Range. From here on it’s a slow, tortuous ascent, climbing

words.definition: anguages）镇域中，正航；的英文教肇方法，主要有文法翻器法（Grammar Translation ）、句型楝霄法；(Pattern Practice）、濡 jfil 法（Communicative Approach）、阔章法（Reading；Approach）、棕合法（General Approach） 等五程。 以下分别在明
